task_completion.description: Open the dwanko_run.tnz scene in OpenToonz
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application window is open and the 'dwanko_run' scene is loaded, visible in the title bar, xsheet, or timeline.
The 'Output Settings' dialog is open with the camera/output resolution Width set to 1280 and Height set to 720.

task_completion.description: Set the render frame range from 1 to 16
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Output Settings' dialog shows the 'From' frame set to 1 and the 'To' frame set to 16.

task_completion.description: Set the output directory to the web_trim folder and format to PNG
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Output Settings' dialog shows the 'Save In' path set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/web_trim/' (or a matching relative path) and the file format dropdown set to 'png'.
A render progress bar is visible in OpenToonz, or a file manager/terminal window shows the resulting 16 PNG files successfully created in the web_trim directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to configure and render the scene
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with OpenToonz menus and the Output Settings dialog, rather than using terminal commands (like ffmpeg) to process the files.

integrity.description: The rendered files are genuine outputs from OpenToonz
integrity.visual_evidence: The render progress window is shown, or the file manager displays newly created PNG files with thumbnails matching the animation, rather than empty files or copied stock images.

integrity.description: The agent did not write a script to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editor or terminal is shown executing a python/bash script to modify the .tnz XML file directly or generate dummy files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source scene is exactly '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz'. The target output directory is '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/web_trim'. The required resolution is 1280x720. The required frame range is 1 to 16 (totaling exactly 16 frames). The output format must be PNG.
